businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla has expanded beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin reached a 2-year high of 20.1%. FSD has shifted to a $99/month subscription model to maximize recurring revenue. Key catalysts for April 2026 include Cybercab production start and Roadster unveil.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 16x the S&P 500 average (22x) and 23x the auto industry average (15x). Extreme premium pricing in future growth from AI, robotaxi, and energy

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS of $2.81. 3.5x more expensive than NVDA (37x)

keyMetrics.name: Revenue (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: -3.1% YoY decline. Auto segment revenue dropped, but energy storage is surging

keyMetrics.name: Net Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Margin pressure from price competition and new business investments. Down from 2024 levels

keyMetrics.name: Free Cash Flow
keyMetrics.interpretation: Insufficient vs. $20B+ planned capex. Major investment cycle underway

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Below S&P 500 average (18%) due to massive capital expenditures

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $20-25B Joint Semiconductor Factory Launched (3/21)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI jointly investing $20-25B in a semiconductor factory in Austin. Targeting 1 terawatt computing power annually, with mass production from 2027. Some analysts question feasibility given only TSMC, Samsung, and Intel operate at this scale

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Starting April
growthDrivers.description: Steering wheel-free fully autonomous robotaxi. NHTSA approval in progress. Production start opens a new revenue stream

growthDrivers.title: Roadster Unveil Expected April
growthDrivers.description: Musk announced on March 17 that the Roadster will be unveiled next month, calling it 'the best ever.' Brand halo effect expected

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Deal
growthDrivers.description: Next-gen AI chip production deal with Samsung. Reducing dependence on Nvidia through in-house AI infrastructure

growthDrivers.title: FSD Subscription Model ($99/mo)
growthDrivers.description: Shifted from $12K one-time to $99/month subscription. Maximizing recurring revenue based on 8.4 billion miles of driving data

growthDrivers.title: China Sales Surge (+35% YoY)
growthDrivers.description: Jan-Feb 2026 China sales up 35% year-over-year. Sustained growth in the world's largest EV market

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles of FSD data, NACS charging standard dominance, Samsung AI6 foundry deal for AI chip internalization

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD driving miles. An insurmountable scale of autonomous driving training data
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM and others now use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantage and rapid innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and loyal fanbase drive demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China market leader, catching Tesla in global EV sales volume. Price competitiveness advantage

competitors.name: Rivian
competitors.detail: $1B+ robotaxi partnership with Uber (3/19). Plans 10,000 R2 autonomous vehicles for SF and Miami by 2028

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in autonomous driving commercialization. Expanding robotaxi services

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV market share with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: TSLA rebounds 3.5% to $380.83 on Terafab semiconductor factory news
recentNews.significance: Tesla, SpaceX, xAI joint semiconductor factory news offset analyst downgrade headwinds

recentNews.headline: UBS slashes Q1 2026 delivery forecast to 345,000 units, down 18%
recentNews.significance: Citing 'persistent demand winter' in China and Germany, 7% below consensus of 371,000

recentNews.headline: NHTSA elevates FSD probe to engineering analysis phase
recentNews.significance: Intensified regulatory scrutiny adds uncertainty to Cybercab launch timeline

recentNews.headline: Tesla pursues $2.9B Chinese-made solar equipment purchase
recentNews.significance: Aggressive investment in solar capacity expansion, signaling energy business commitment

recentNews.headline: Musk found liable by jury for misleading Twitter investors during acquisition
recentNews.significance: California jury ruled Musk defrauded Twitter shareholders during $44B acquisition. Limited direct impact on TSLA but adds to legal overhang

analystOpinions.notableComment: UBS maintains Sell with $352 target. Projects Q1 deliveries of 345,000, an 18% decline from prior quarter, warning of a 'demand winter'

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Probe Elevated to Engineering Analysis
risks.description: Regulatory deep dive into FSD safety has begun. A recall order or feature restriction could significantly delay Cybercab production timeline

risks.title: Q1 2026 Delivery Shortfall Risk
risks.description: UBS projects 345,000 units, down 18% from Q4 2025's 421,000. May miss consensus of 371,000

risks.title: Extreme Valuation (P/E 351x)
risks.description: TTM P/E of 351x is extreme by any standard. Significant downside risk if results disappoint

risks.title: Intensifying Competition from BYD, Xiaomi
risks.description: Low-cost EV competitors gaining share in China and Europe

risks.title: $20B+ Capex Burden
risks.description: Massive capital investments in Terafab and Cybercab factories pressure FCF. Payback period needed

buyReasons.title: Terafab Semiconductor Ecosystem Entry
buyReasons.rationale: The $20-25B joint semiconductor factory represents a new growth axis in AI chip self-production. Success would reduce Nvidia dependence and enable cost innovation

buyReasons.title: April Cybercab/Roadster Dual Catalysts
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production start and Roadster unveil both in April. Autonomous commercialization and brand premium catalysts

buyReasons.title: RSI 34 Oversold Territory
buyReasons.rationale: Down 24% from 52-week high with RSI at 34. Technically oversold with elevated rebound probability

buyReasons.title: Energy Business High Growth
buyReasons.rationale: Record Megapack deployments and $2.9B solar equipment purchase driving energy revenue growth

overallOpinion: UBS cut Q1 delivery forecast to 345,000 units (down 18%), and NHTSA elevated its FSD probe to engineering analysis, compounding headwinds.
Terafab semiconductor factory (Tesla, SpaceX, xAI joint venture, $20-25B) and April Cybercab production start are the keys to recovery. TSLA rebounded 3.5% on 3/23 on this news.
Down 24% from 52-week high with RSI at 34 in oversold territory. Trading below its 200-day average ($392), with analyst target of $422 (+10.7%) requiring Q1 results and Cybercab progress.

investmentVerdict.summary: Q1 delivery decline concerns and NHTSA FSD probe escalation weigh on the near term. However, Terafab launch, April Cybercab production, and oversold RSI provide rebound catalysts. A 'wait then scale in' approach is appropriate. P/E of 351x is hard to justify without an earnings surprise at the 4/21 report.

sources.description: 3/23 price action and Terafab semiconductor factory news

sources.description: Technical indicators, valuation metrics, analyst consensus

sources.description: NHTSA, Rivian, and European sales triple headwinds

threads.hook: UBS cuts delivery forecast 18%, but semiconductor factory news sparks rebound
threads.text:
$TSLA (3/24) $380.83 (+3.50%)

UBS cut Q1 deliveries to 345K (-18%) and NHTSA elevated the FSD probe to engineering analysis. Headwinds are stacking up.

But Terafab (Tesla, SpaceX, xAI joint venture, $20-25B) launched on 3/21, flipping the narrative. A 3.5% rebound on Monday.

Down 24% from 52-week high with RSI at 34, deeply oversold. Analyst target $422 (+10.7%). April 21 earnings and Cybercab production will set the direction.

witty.hook: Tesla's making chips now? Meanwhile, about those car deliveries...
witty.text:
$TSLA (3/24) UBS: "You'll only sell 345K cars." Tesla: "We're making semiconductors now." Market: +3.5%. $380.83.

1/4
thread:
NHTSA wants a closer look at FSD. Hard to sell a car with no steering wheel when regulators are sharpening their pencils.

2/4

Terafab: $20-25B to make your own chips. Bold move. Small detail: FCF is $3.7B. The math is creative.

3/4

Down 24% from highs. Cheap? At 351x P/E, 'cheap' is relative. April 21 earnings will be the verdict.

4/4

thread:

UBS cut Q1 deliveries to 345K (-18%) and NHTSA elevated the FSD probe to engineering analysis. Headwinds are stacking up.

But Terafab (Tesla, SpaceX, xAI joint venture, $20-25B) launched on 3/21, flipping the narrative. A 3.5% rebound on Monday.

witty.hook: Tesla's making chips now? Meanwhile, about those car deliveries...
witty.text:
$TSLA (3/24) $380.83 (+3.50%)

UBS says Tesla will only sell 345K cars this quarter. Tesla's response? "We're building a $25B semiconductor factory." The market liked the deflection, up 3.5%.

Terafab is ambitious. Making your own AI chips when your FCF is $3.7B and you're planning $20B+ in capex takes a special kind of confidence. NHTSA also wants a deeper look at FSD.

Down 24% from highs but still at 351x P/E. Cheap is in the eye of the beholder. April 21 earnings will be the reckoning.

empathy.hook: TSLA holders, it's been a rough ride lately
empathy.text:
$TSLA (3/24) $380.83 (+3.50%)

If you've been holding Tesla, this month hasn't been kind. Down 24% from the 52-week high. UBS slashing delivery estimates, NHTSA escalating the FSD probe.

But today's 3.5% bounce was a reminder that the story isn't over. Terafab semiconductor factory launched, Cybercab production starts in April, Roadster unveil is coming.

RSI at 34 puts it in oversold territory. Analyst target is $422 (+10.7%). There's no need to make a decision right now. Wait for April 21 earnings.
